arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -E 'llvm|binutils' | head -5

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try to download debug symbols from Microsoft's symbol server using the ELF Build ID
# BuildId: 4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801
BUILDID="4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801"

# Try the Microsoft symbol server with ELF build ID key format
# Format: /libcoreclr.so/elf-buildid-sym-<buildid>/libcoreclr.so
for variant in "elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}" "elf-buildid-${BUILDID}" "${BUILDID}ffffffff" "${BUILDID}"; do
  URL="https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so/${variant}/libcoreclr.so"
  echo "Trying: $URL"
  STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -L "$URL" --max-time 10)
  echo "  Status: $STATUS"
  if [ "$STATUS" = "200" ]; then
    echo "  Found! Downloading..."
    curl -s -L -o /tmp/libcoreclr_sym.so "$URL"
    file /tmp/libcoreclr_sym.so
    break
  fi
done
arguments.description: Download debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Download debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames in this tombstone. The backtrace shows frames from `libcoreclr.so` with a BuildId of `4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801`.

To symbolicate these frames, I need the debug symbols for this specific build of `libcoreclr.so`. The approach would be:

1. Use the BuildId to find and download the matching symbol file from a symbol server (like Microsoft's symbol server)
2. Use a tool like `llvm-symbolizer` or `addr2line` to resolve the addresses


I'm trying to figure out the right symbol server URL format for this ELF binary. Microsoft's symbol server has a specific structure, but for Linux native libraries the format might differ—I should check if the dotnet symbol server or NuGet symbol server would be more appropriate here.

arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -E 'llvm|binutils' | head -5

arguments.command:
BUILDID="4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801"

# Try downloading separate debug info file (_.debug) from Microsoft symbol server
for variant in "elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}" "elf-buildid-${BUILDID}"; do
  URL="https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/${variant}/_.debug"
  echo "Trying _.debug: $URL"
  STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -L "$URL" --max-time 10)
  echo "  Status: $STATUS"
  if [ "$STATUS" = "200" ]; then
    echo "  Found!"
    curl -s -L -o /tmp/libcoreclr_debug /tmp/url_response "$URL"
    break
  fi
done

echo ""

# Try the dotnet symbol server / NuGet symbol server
for server in "https://symbols.nuget.org/download/symbols" "https://dotnetcli.azureedge.net/symbolserver"; do
  for name in "libcoreclr.so" "_.debug"; do
    for variant in "elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}" "elf-buildid-${BUILDID}"; do
      URL="${server}/${name}/${variant}/${name}"
      echo "Trying: $URL"
      STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -L "$URL" --max-time 10)
      echo "  Status: $STATUS"
      if [ "$STATUS" = "200" ] || [ "$STATUS" = "301" ] || [ "$STATUS" = "302" ]; then
        echo "  Found!"
      fi
    done
  done
done

data.reasoningText:
The symbol server didn't have it with those formats. Let me try a few more things:

1. Try the dotnet debuginfod server
2. Try the NuGet symbol server
3. Try different URL formats

Let me also check if there's a `_.debug` file format on the symbol server, which is the common format for separate debug info files.

Actually, this Build ID looks like it might be for a specific .NET Android runtime build. The Microsoft symbol server for Linux/Android ELF binaries might use a different format. Let me try:
- `https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-<buildid>/_.debug`


- The NuGet symbol server at `https://symbols.nuget.org/download/symbols/`

I should also check the dotnet debuginfod endpoint as another option.
